title: Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Tunisian dish made with lamb, dry white wine, and coconut cream, resulting in a jammy and crumbly texture, this recipe is halal-friendly and perfect for meal prep. It serves as a delicious and flavorful dinner option.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the lamb cubes and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the dry white wine, coconut cream, ground cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, stirring to combine.
Cover the Dutch oven and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving.
